summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Anxiety and breast changes.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing anxiety, particularly during the day at work, with a significant exacerbation in November and December. She notes that anxiety spiked randomly, unrelated to specific triggers. She attributes this to the stress of delivering covid test results in the early pandemic and felt selfish not being on the front lines. She also reports feeling guilty and mentions irritability before starting progesterone. The patient's anxiety was linked to her period, worsening before her period and then spiking randomly. She discontinued Camila birth control and reports increased appetite. She has been concerned about her breasts for some time, noting lumps that have persisted despite progesterone use. She is worried about her family history of breast cancer and her husband's reluctance to receive the vaccine. The patient has been actively involved in vaccine education at home.

Findings
Examination reveals palpable breast nodules, described as denser in one breast and causing discomfort with palpation. The patient's masked face appears thinner. Vital signs are within normal limits. The patient reports no weight loss but has experienced increased hunger. The patient's pap was negative in 2019, and she is willing to repeat the test.

Assessment
Clinical presentation is most consistent with persistent breast changes, potentially related to hormonal fluctuations and exacerbated by stress. Further evaluation with a breast specialist is warranted.

Plan
Order a referral to a breast specialist for further evaluation and management. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to monitor the breast nodules and assess the patient's concerns. Refill the topical corticosteroid cream. Advise the patient to continue with her current vaccination efforts and encourage her husband and daughter to receive the vaccine.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Follow-up bilateral breast cysts.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Donna Torres is a 40-year-old female who presents for follow-up surveillance of bilateral breast cysts.

The patient reports continual presence of “lumps” in bilateral breasts with some soreness. She is being seen today for surveillance of the cysts. The patient is taking progesterone, which was initially prescribed to support normalization of menstruation, which was effective. She is also still using vaginal cream one time per week which has improved symptoms of itching and discomfort.

Her last pap smear was in 2019, with negative/negative results. She did have an abnormal pap smear in 2009, which resulted in repeat pap smears per 6 months until her results normalized.

Mrs. Torres experienced increased anxiety in 11/2021 and 12/2021, stating “it was brutal” and felt she could not manage her symptoms and sought treatment. Dr. Brown started the patient on Buspar, which has successfully controlled her anxiety. The patient relates her anxiety to work related stress and possibly aging. Her work involves supporting Covid evaluation centers and clinics. She confirms receiving both Covid vaccinations. Initially, she was experiencing spikes in anxiety prior to menstruation, but her anxiety then became more random with an unclear cause. To note, the patient was started on Singulair, which the patient was told could also cause anxiety.

The patient discontinued Camila birth control, which did result in a significant increase in appetite. This has resolved and she reports she has lost weight in the past 6 months.

Gynecologic History
Last pap smear: 2019.
History of abnormal pap smear: 2009.

PAST HISTORY

Medical
Bilateral breast cysts.
Anxiety.

SOCIAL HISTORY

Employed in healthcare, supports Covid evaluation centers and clinics.

FAMILY HISTORY

Mother, deceased age 45, from complications of breast cancer.

CURRENT MEDICATIONS

Buspar.
Singulair.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Genitourinary
Breast: Breast density.

ASSESSMENT

• Follow-up bilateral breast cysts.
• Pap smear.

PLAN

Follow-up bilateral breast cysts
Physical exam indicated stable breast density bilaterally. The patient is being seen regularly for monitoring. The plan is to continue close monitoring and the patient will be referred to breast specialist for additional review. The patient agreed to this plan.

Pap smear
Exam was normal. No additional treatment needed at this time.

INSTRUCTIONS

Schedule appointment with breast specialist. Call clinic with any additional concerns.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's masked face appears thinner.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a subjective appearance comparison without objective measurement; it reads more like reported history than a clear exam finding and could be better placed in HPI, though it is not seriously misleading where it is.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports no weight loss but has experienced increased hunger.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are patient-reported symptoms and history rather than objective exam or test results, so they fit better in HPI, though their presence in Findings is not highly misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's pap was negative in 2019, and she is willing to repeat the test.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is prior history and patient preference rather than a current exam or test result; it belongs more appropriately in HPI, but its placement in Findings does not substantially distort clinical meaning.
